_comment: This is the ONLY file you should need to edit. 'paths' tells the suite where your binaries/source live; 'implemented' tells it which flags your mkdir supports so it only runs applicable cases. Everything else has a sensible default.

paths._comment: oracle_bin: a GNU coreutils mkdir binary (used only to regenerate/extend suites/ and for live differential fuzzing -- NOT needed just to judge a candidate against the committed suites/). On Linux this is usually /usr/bin/mkdir already. On macOS the system /bin/mkdir is BSD, not GNU -- install GNU coreutils (e.g. 'brew install coreutils') and point this at the gnubin mkdir, e.g. /opt/homebrew/opt/coreutils/libexec/gnubin/mkdir.
paths._comment2: candidate_bin: your normal (non-instrumented) build. Required for run_all.sh / runner.py. For example: agentic_cyber/src/new_mkdir/new_mkdir.c
paths._comment3: candidate_asan_bin: an ASan/UBSan-instrumented build of your mkdir. If you already build this yourself (any language/toolchain), just put its path here and leave candidate_src empty -- run_all.sh will use it directly without trying to compile anything. If you want run_all.sh to build it FOR you from a single C source file, fill in candidate_src/cc/cc_flags below instead and leave this as the output path.
paths._comment4: candidate_src / cc / cc_flags: only used by build_asan.sh, only if your mkdir is a single C source file you want this suite to compile with sanitizers. Leave candidate_src empty to skip auto-build (bring your own candidate_asan_bin instead).

_comment5: implemented: flag IDs your binary currently supports. A generated case only runs if every ID in its own 'flags' list appears here. Short flags use their two-char form ('-p', '-m', ...); long-only flags use their full name. The example below is a placeholder -- replace it with your own implemented flags.
